001|# Product Requirement Document: Multithreaded Appium Automation for Android
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python
006|- **Project Name:** multithreaded_appium_android
007|- **Restated Requirements:**
008|  - Develop a Python program that runs multiple multithreaded Appium instances to perform UI interactions on Android devices.
009|  - The program must optimize the existing codebase and address significant bugs.
010|  - Requirements must include scalability, reliability, and performance improvements.
011|
012|## 2. Product Definition
013|### Product Goals
014|1. Enable concurrent UI automation on multiple Android devices using multithreaded Appium instances.
015|2. Optimize code for maximum performance and resource efficiency.
016|3. Ensure high reliability and scalability for large-scale automated testing.
017|### User Stories
018|1. As a QA engineer, I want to run automated UI tests on multiple Android devices in parallel so that I can reduce testing time and increase coverage.
019|2. As a developer, I want the program to automatically recover from Appium or device failures so that test runs are reliable and require minimal manual intervention.
020|3. As a test manager, I want to scale the number of concurrent Appium sessions based on available hardware so that I can optimize resource usage and test throughput.
021|4. As a DevOps engineer, I want to monitor the performance and status of all running Appium instances so that I can quickly identify and resolve bottlenecks or failures.
022|
023|### Competitive Analysis
024|| Product                        | Pros                                              | Cons                                             |
025||-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
026|| Appium Grid                   | Scalable, supports multiple devices, open-source   | Complex setup, resource intensive                |
027|| Selenium Grid                 | Mature, parallel execution, good reporting         | Limited mobile support, less Android focus       |
028|| Kobiton                       | Cloud-based, easy device management                | Paid, limited customization                      |
029|| Sauce Labs                    | Scalable, cloud-based, strong analytics            | Expensive, less control over infrastructure      |
030|| TestProject                   | Free, easy setup, supports parallel execution      | Limited advanced features, sunset announced      |
031|| BrowserStack App Automate     | Large device cloud, easy integration               | Costly, limited local device support             |
032|| Custom Python Appium Scripts  | Flexible, full control, open-source                | Requires manual scaling, error-prone             |
033|
034|### Competitive Quadrant Chart
035|```mermaid
036|quadrantChart
037|    title "Android UI Automation Solutions"
038|    x-axis "Low Scalability" --> "High Scalability"
039|    y-axis "Low Reliability" --> "High Reliability"
040|    quadrant-1 "Niche Solutions"
041|    quadrant-2 "Emerging Players"
042|    quadrant-3 "Legacy Tools"
043|    quadrant-4 "Market Leaders"
044|    "Appium Grid": [0.8, 0.7]
045|    "Selenium Grid": [0.6, 0.6]
046|    "Kobiton": [0.7, 0.8]

049|    "BrowserStack App Automate": [0.85, 0.85]

001|# Product Requirement Document: Multithreaded Appium Automation for Android
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python
006|- **Project Name:** multithreaded_appium_android
007|- **Restated Requirements:**
008|  - Develop a Python program that runs multiple multithreaded Appium instances to perform UI interactions on Android devices.
009|  - The program must optimize the existing codebase and address significant bugs.
010|  - Requirements must include scalability, reliability, and performance improvements.
011|
012|## 2. Product Definition
013|### Product Goals
014|1. Enable concurrent UI automation on multiple Android devices using multithreaded Appium instances.
015|2. Optimize code for maximum performance and resource efficiency.
016|3. Ensure high reliability and scalability for large-scale automated testing.
017|### User Stories
018|1. As a QA engineer, I want to run automated UI tests on multiple Android devices in parallel so that I can reduce testing time and increase coverage.
019|2. As a developer, I want the program to automatically recover from Appium or device failures so that test runs are reliable and require minimal manual intervention.
020|3. As a test manager, I want to scale the number of concurrent Appium sessions based on available hardware so that I can optimize resource usage and test throughput.
021|4. As a DevOps engineer, I want to monitor the performance and status of all running Appium instances so that I can quickly identify and resolve bottlenecks or failures.
022|
023|### Competitive Analysis
024|| Product                        | Pros                                              | Cons                                             |
025||-------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
026|| Appium Grid                   | Scalable, supports multiple devices, open-source   | Complex setup, resource intensive                |
027|| Selenium Grid                 | Mature, parallel execution, good reporting         | Limited mobile support, less Android focus       |
028|| Kobiton                       | Cloud-based, easy device management                | Paid, limited customization                      |
029|| Sauce Labs                    | Scalable, cloud-based, strong analytics            | Expensive, less control over infrastructure      |
030|| TestProject                   | Free, easy setup, supports parallel execution      | Limited advanced features, sunset announced      |
031|| BrowserStack App Automate     | Large device cloud, easy integration               | Costly, limited local device support             |
032|| Custom Python Appium Scripts  | Flexible, full control, open-source                | Requires manual scaling, error-prone             |
033|
034|### Competitive Quadrant Chart
035|```mermaid
036|quadrantChart
037|    title "Android UI Automation Solutions"
038|    x-axis "Low Scalability" --> "High Scalability"
039|    y-axis "Low Reliability" --> "High Reliability"
040|    quadrant-1 "Niche Solutions"
041|    quadrant-2 "Emerging Players"
042|    quadrant-3 "Legacy Tools"
043|    quadrant-4 "Market Leaders"
044|    "Appium Grid": [0.8, 0.7]
045|    "Selenium Grid": [0.6, 0.6]
046|    "Kobiton": [0.7, 0.8]
047|    "Sauce Labs": [0.9, 0.9]
048|    "TestProject": [0.5, 0.5]
049|    "BrowserStack App Automate": [0.85, 0.85]
050|    "Custom Python Appium": [0.4, 0.4]
051|    "Our Target Product": [0.95, 0.95]
052|```
053|## 3. Technical Specifications
054|### Requirements Analysis
055|- The program must support launching and managing multiple Appium server instances in parallel using Python multithreading.
056|- Each Appium instance should be able to connect to a unique Android device or emulator and execute UI interaction scripts independently.
057|- The system must handle device/Appium failures gracefully, with automatic retries and error logging.
058|- Resource usage (CPU, memory, network) must be optimized to maximize throughput and minimize bottlenecks.
059|- The codebase must be refactored to eliminate major bugs, improve maintainability, and support future enhancements.
060|- The solution should be easily configurable for different device pools, test scripts, and concurrency levels.
061|- Real-time monitoring and reporting of test execution status, device health, and performance metrics are required.
062|
063|### Requirements Pool
064|- **P0 (Must-have):**
065|  - Multithreaded management of Appium instances
066|  - Robust error handling and recovery
067|  - Optimized resource usage
068|  - Support for multiple Android devices/emulators
069|  - Real-time status and performance reporting
070|  - Major bug fixes in existing codebase
071|- **P1 (Should-have):**
072|  - Configurable device pools and test scripts
073|  - Scalable architecture for large device farms
074|  - Integration with CI/CD pipelines
075|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**
076|  - Web-based dashboard for monitoring
077|  - Historical test result analytics
078|  - Support for iOS devices
079|
080|### UI Design Draft
081|- **Main Interface:**
082|  - Dashboard showing active Appium sessions, device status, and test progress
083|  - Controls to start/stop sessions, configure device pools, and select test scripts
084|  - Real-time logs and error notifications
085|- **Reporting:**
086|  - Summary view of test results, device health, and performance metrics
087|
088|### Open Questions
089|- What is the maximum number of concurrent devices required?
090|- Are there specific Android versions or device models to support?
091|- What are the most critical bugs in the existing codebase?
092|- Is integration with external test management or CI/CD tools needed?
093|- What level of reporting and analytics is expected by stakeholders?

The system will leverage the Appium-Python-Client for device interaction, and psutil for resource monitoring. Logging and error handling will be implemented using Python's logging module. The architecture will be modular, separating device/session management, test execution, monitoring, and configuration. Code will be optimized for thread safety, resource usage, and maintainability. The system will leverage the Appium-Python-Client for device interaction, and psutil for resource monitoring. Logging and error handling will be implemented using Python\'s logging module. The architecture will be modular, separating device/session management, test execution, monitoring, and configuration. Code will be optimized for thread safety, resource usage, and maintainability. Real-time status reporting will be provided via a CLI or optional web dashboard (Flask-based, if needed). Scalability is achieved by abstracting device pools and session managers, allowing easy extension to more devices or integration with CI/CD.\\n\\n## File list\\n\\n- main.py\\n- config.py\\n- device_manager.py\\n- session_manager.py\\n- test_runner.py\\n- monitor.py\\n- logger.py\\n- utils.py\\n- requirements.txt\\n- docs/system_design.md\\n\\n## Data structures and interfaces:\\n\\n```mermaid\\nclassDiagram\\n    class Config {\\n        +devices: list[DeviceConfig]\\n        +test_scripts: list[str]\\n        +max_concurrent_sessions: int\\n        +load(path: str) -> Config\\n    }\\n    class DeviceConfig {\\n        +device_id: str\\n        +platform_version: str\\n        +model: str\\n        +appium_url: str\\n    }\\n    class DeviceManager {\\n        +devices: dict[str, DeviceConfig]\\n        +discover_devices() -> None\\n        +get_available_devices() -> list[DeviceConfig]\\n    }\\n    class SessionManager {\\n        +sessions: dict[str, AppiumSession]\\n        +start_session(device: DeviceConfig) -> AppiumSession\\n        +stop_session(session_id: str) -> None\\n        +restart_session(session_id: str) -> AppiumSession\\n        +handle_failure(session_id: str, error: Exception) -> None\\n    }\\n    class AppiumSession {\\n        +session_id: str\\n        +device: DeviceConfig\\n        +driver: WebDriver\\n        +run_test(test_script: str) -> TestResult\\n        +status: str\\n    }\\n    class TestRunner {\\n        +run_tests(sessions: list[AppiumSession], test_scripts: list[str]) -> dict[str, TestResult]\\n    }\\n    class Monitor {\\n        +collect_metrics() -> dict\\n        +report_status() -> None\\n        +log_event(event: str) -> None\\n    }\\n    class Logger {\\n        +log(msg: str, level: str) -> None\\n        +log_error(error: Exception) -> None\\n    }\\n    class TestResult {\\n        +session_id: str\\n        +success: bool\\n        +details: str\\n        +timestamp: datetime\\n    }\\n    Config <|-- DeviceConfig\\n    DeviceManager o-- DeviceConfig\\n    SessionManager o-- AppiumSession\\n    AppiumSession o-- DeviceConfig\\n    TestRunner o-- AppiumSession\\n    Monitor o-- AppiumSession\\n    Logger <.. AppiumSession\\n    TestResult <.. AppiumSession\\n```\\n\\n## Program call flow:\\n\\n```mermaid\\nsequenceDiagram\\n    participant M as main.py\\n    participant C as Config\\n    participant DM as DeviceManager\\n    participant SM as SessionManager\\n    participant TR as TestRunner\\n    participant MON as Monitor\\n    participant LOG as Logger\\n    M->>C: load(path)\\n    M->>DM: discover_devices()\\n    DM-->>M: get_available_devices()\\n    M->>SM: start_session(device) (for each device)\\n    SM-->>M: AppiumSession\\n    M->>TR: run_tests(sessions, test_scripts)\\n    TR->>AppiumSession: run_test(test_script)\\n    AppiumSession-->>TR: TestResult\\n    TR-->>M: dict[session_id, TestResult]\\n    M->>MON: collect_metrics()\\n    MON->>M: report_status()\\n    SM->>LOG: log_error(error) (on failure)\\n    SM->>SM: handle_failure(session_id, error)\\n    SM->>SM: restart_session(session_id) (if needed)\\n    M->>SM: stop_session(session_id) (on completion)\\n```\\n\\n## Anything UNCLEAR\\n\\n- Maximum number of concurrent devices required?\\n- Specific Android versions or device models to support?\\n- Details of the most critical bugs in the existing codebase?\\n- Is integration with external test management or CI/CD tools needed?\\n- What level of reporting and analytics is expected by stakeholders?\\n"\n        }\n    }\n]\n```\n'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': "Command Editor.write executed: The writing/coding the of the file system_design.md' is now completed. 2025-9-20 14:23:40.634 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/multithreaded_appium_android/main.py has been successfully created, with content:
import sys
import threading
import time
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed

from config import Config
from device_manager import DeviceManager
from session_manager import SessionManager
from test_runner import TestRunner
from monitor import Monitor
from logger import Logger

def main():
    # Initialize logger
    logger = Logger()
    logger.log("Starting multithreaded Appium automation system.", "INFO")

    # Load configuration
    try:
        config_path = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "config.yaml"
        config = Config.load(config_path)
        logger.log(f"Loaded configuration from {config_path}", "INFO")
    except Exception as e:
        logger.log_error(e)
        sys.exit(1)

    # Discover devices
    device_manager = DeviceManager(config.devices)
    try:
        device_manager.discover_devices()
        available_devices = device_manager.get_available_devices()
        logger.log(f"Discovered {len(available_devices)} available devices.", "INFO")
    except Exception as e:
        logger.log_error(e)
        sys.exit(1)

    if not available_devices:
        logger.log("No available devices found. Exiting.", "ERROR")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Initialize session manager
    session_manager = SessionManager()
    sessions = []
    session_threads = []
    session_lock = threading.Lock()

    # Start Appium sessions for each device (up to max_concurrent_sessions)
    max_sessions = min(config.max_concurrent_sessions, len(available_devices))
    logger.log(f"Starting up to {max_sessions} Appium sessions.", "INFO")

    def start_session_thread(device):
        try:
            session = session_manager.start_session(device)
            with session_lock:
                sessions.append(session)
            logger.log(f"Started session {session.session_id} for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.log_error(e)
            session_manager.handle_failure(device.device_id, e)

    threads = []
    for device in available_devices[:max_sessions]:
        t = threading.Thread(target=start_session_thread, args=(device,))
        t.start()
        threads.append(t)

    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    if not sessions:
        logger.log("No sessions started successfully. Exiting.", "ERROR")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Initialize test runner
    test_runner = TestRunner()
    test_scripts = config.test_scripts

    # Run tests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor
    logger.log("Running tests on all sessions.", "INFO")
    test_results = {}
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_sessions) as executor:
        future_to_session = {
            executor.submit(test_runner.run_tests, [session], test_scripts): session.session_id
            for session in sessions
        }
        for future in as_completed(future_to_session):
            session_id = future_to_session[future]
            try:
                result_dict = future.result()
                test_results.update(result_dict)
                logger.log(f"Test completed for session {session_id}.", "INFO")
            except Exception as e:
                logger.log_error(e)
                session_manager.handle_failure(session_id, e)

    # Monitor system metrics and report status
    monitor = Monitor(sessions)
    try:
        metrics = monitor.collect_metrics()
        monitor.report_status()
        logger.log(f"System metrics: {metrics}", "INFO")
    except Exception as e:
        logger.log_error(e)

    # Stop all sessions
    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")
    for session in sessions:
        try:
            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)
            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.log_error(e)

    # Print summary of test results
    logger.log("Test Results Summary:", "INFO")
    for session_id, result in test_results.items():
        logger.log(
            f"Session {session_id}: Success={result.success}, Details={result.details}, Timestamp={result.timestamp}",
            "INFO"
        )

    logger.log("Multithreaded Appium automation system finished.", "INFO")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize session manager\n    session_manager = SessionManager()\n    sessions = []\n    session_threads = []\n    session_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    # Start Appium sessions for each device (up to max_concurrent_sessions)\n    max_sessions = min(config.max_concurrent_sessions, len(available_devices))\n    logger.log(f"Starting up to {max_sessions} Appium sessions.", "INFO")\n\n    def start_session_thread(device):\n        try:\n            session = session_manager.start_session(device)\n            with session_lock:\n                sessions.append(session)\n            logger.log(f"Started session {session.session_id} for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n            session_manager.handle_failure(device.device_id, e)\n\n    threads = []\n    for device in available_devices[:max_sessions]:\n        t = threading.Thread(target=start_session_thread, args=(device,))\n        t.start()\n        threads.append(t)\n\n    for t in threads:\n        t.join()\n\n    if not sessions:\n        logger.log("No sessions started successfully. Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize test runner\n    test_runner = TestRunner()\n    test_scripts = config.test_scripts\n\n    # Run tests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor\n    logger.log("Running tests on all sessions.", "INFO")\n    test_results = {}\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_sessions) as executor:\n        future_to_session = {\n            executor.submit(test_runner.run_tests, [session], test_scripts): session.session_id\n            for session in sessions\n        }\n        for future in as_completed(future_to_session):\n            session_id = future_to_session[future]\n            try:\n                result_dict = future.result()\n                test_results.update(result_dict)\n                logger.log(f"Test completed for session {session_id}.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.log_error(e)\n                session_manager.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n\n    # Monitor system metrics and report status\n    monitor = Monitor(sessions)\n    try:\n        metrics = monitor.collect_metrics()\n        monitor.report_status()\n        logger.log(f"System metrics: {metrics}", "INFO")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Print summary of test results\n    logger.log("Test Results Summary:", "INFO")\n    for session_id, result in test_results.items():\n        logger.log(\n            f"Session {session_id}: Success={result.success}, Details={result.details}, Timestamp={result.timestamp}",\n            "INFO"\n        )\n\n    logger.log("Multithreaded Appium automation system finished.", "INFO")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    main()'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of main.py is complete and matches the requirements outlined in the current task. Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize session manager\n    session_manager = SessionManager()\n    sessions = []\n    session_threads = []\n    session_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    # Start Appium sessions for each device (up to max_concurrent_sessions)\n    max_sessions = min(config.max_concurrent_sessions, len(available_devices))\n    logger.log(f"Starting up to {max_sessions} Appium sessions.", "INFO")\n\n    def start_session_thread(device):\n        try:\n            session = session_manager.start_session(device)\n            with session_lock:\n                sessions.append(session)\n            logger.log(f"Started session {session.session_id} for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n            session_manager.handle_failure(device.device_id, e)\n\n    threads = []\n    for device in available_devices[:max_sessions]:\n        t = threading.Thread(target=start_session_thread, args=(device,))\n        t.start()\n        threads.append(t)\n\n    for t in threads:\n        t.join()\n\n    if not sessions:\n        logger.log("No sessions started successfully. Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize test runner\n    test_runner = TestRunner()\n    test_scripts = config.test_scripts\n\n    # Run tests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor\n    logger.log("Running tests on all sessions.", "INFO")\n    test_results = {}\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_sessions) as executor:\n        future_to_session = {\n            executor.submit(test_runner.run_tests, [session], test_scripts): session.session_id\n            for session in sessions\n        }\n        for future in as_completed(future_to_session):\n            session_id = future_to_session[future]\n            try:\n                result_dict = future.result()\n                test_results.update(result_dict)\n                logger.log(f"Test completed for session {session_id}.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.log_error(e)\n                session_manager.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n\n    # Monitor system metrics and report status\n    monitor = Monitor(sessions)\n    try:\n        metrics = monitor.collect_metrics()\n        monitor.report_status()\n        logger.log(f"System metrics: {metrics}", "INFO")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Print summary of test results\n    logger.log("Test Results Summary:", "INFO")\n    for session_id, result in test_results.items():\n        logger.log(\n            f"Session {session_id}: Success={result.success}, Details={result.details}, Timestamp={result.timestamp}",\n            "INFO"\n        )\n\n    logger.log("Multithreaded Appium automation system finished.", "INFO")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    main()'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of main.py is complete and matches the requirements outlined in the current task. import os
from typing import List, Optional

class DeviceConfig:
    def __init__(self, device_id: str, platform_version: str, model: str, appium_url: str):
        self.device_id = device_id
        self.platform_version = platform_version
        self.model = model
        self.appium_url = appium_url

    def __repr__(self):
        return (f"DeviceConfig(device_id={self.device_id}, platform_version={self.platform_version}, "
                f"model={self.model}, appium_url={self.appium_url})")

class Config:
    def __init__(self, devices: List[DeviceConfig], test_scripts: List[str], max_concurrent_sessions: int):
        self.devices = devices
        self.test_scripts = test_scripts
        self.max_concurrent_sessions = max_concurrent_sessions

    @staticmethod
    def load(path: str) -> 'Config':
        if not os.path.exists(path):
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Configuration file not found: {path}")

        with open(path, 'r') as f:
            config_data = yaml.safe_load(f)

        # Validate and parse devices
        devices = []
        devices_data = config_data.get('devices', [])
        if not isinstance(devices_data, list):
            raise ValueError("Config 'devices' must be a list.")

        for device in devices_data:
            required_keys = ['device_id', 'platform_version', 'model', 'appium_url']
            for key in required_keys:
                if key not in device:
                    raise ValueError(f"Device config missing required key: {key}")
            devices.append(DeviceConfig(
                device_id=device['device_id'],
                platform_version=device['platform_version'],
                model=device['model'],
                appium_url=device['appium_url']
            ))

        # Validate and parse test scripts
        test_scripts = config_data.get('test_scripts', [])
        if not isinstance(test_scripts, list):
            raise ValueError("Config 'test_scripts' must be a list.")

        # Parse max_concurrent_sessions
        max_concurrent_sessions = config_data.get('max_concurrent_sessions', 1)
        if not isinstance(max_concurrent_sessions, int) or max_concurrent_sessions < 1:
            raise ValueError("Config 'max_concurrent_sessions' must be a positive integer.")

        return Config(
            devices=devices,
            test_scripts=test_scripts,
            max_concurrent_sessions=max_concurrent_sessions
        )

Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize session manager\n    session_manager = SessionManager()\n    sessions = []\n    session_threads = []\n    session_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    # Start Appium sessions for each device (up to max_concurrent_sessions)\n    max_sessions = min(config.max_concurrent_sessions, len(available_devices))\n    logger.log(f"Starting up to {max_sessions} Appium sessions.", "INFO")\n\n    def start_session_thread(device):\n        try:\n            session = session_manager.start_session(device)\n            with session_lock:\n                sessions.append(session)\n            logger.log(f"Started session {session.session_id} for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n            session_manager.handle_failure(device.device_id, e)\n\n    threads = []\n    for device in available_devices[:max_sessions]:\n        t = threading.Thread(target=start_session_thread, args=(device,))\n        t.start()\n        threads.append(t)\n\n    for t in threads:\n        t.join()\n\n    if not sessions:\n        logger.log("No sessions started successfully. Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize test runner\n    test_runner = TestRunner()\n    test_scripts = config.test_scripts\n\n    # Run tests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor\n    logger.log("Running tests on all sessions.", "INFO")\n    test_results = {}\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_sessions) as executor:\n        future_to_session = {\n            executor.submit(test_runner.run_tests, [session], test_scripts): session.session_id\n            for session in sessions\n        }\n        for future in as_completed(future_to_session):\n            session_id = future_to_session[future]\n            try:\n                result_dict = future.result()\n                test_results.update(result_dict)\n                logger.log(f"Test completed for session {session_id}.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.log_error(e)\n                session_manager.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n\n    # Monitor system metrics and report status\n    monitor = Monitor(sessions)\n    try:\n        metrics = monitor.collect_metrics()\n        monitor.report_status()\n        logger.log(f"System metrics: {metrics}", "INFO")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Print summary of test results\n    logger.log("Test Results Summary:", "INFO")\n    for session_id, result in test_results.items():\n        logger.log(\n            f"Session {session_id}: Success={result.success}, Details={result.details}, Timestamp={result.timestamp}",\n            "INFO"\n        )\n\n    logger.log("Multithreaded Appium automation system finished.", "INFO")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    main()'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of main.py is complete and matches the requirements outlined in the current task. Validate and parse devices\n        devices = []\n        devices_data = config_data.get(\'devices\', [])\n        if not isinstance(devices_data, list):\n            raise ValueError("Config \'devices\' must be a list.")\n\n        for device in devices_data:\n            required_keys = [\'device_id\', \'platform_version\', \'model\', \'appium_url\']\n            for key in required_keys:\n                if key not in device:\n                    raise ValueError(f"Device config missing required key: {key}")\n            devices.append(DeviceConfig(\n                device_id=device[\'device_id\'],\n                platform_version=device[\'platform_version\'],\n                model=device[\'model\'],\n                appium_url=device[\'appium_url\']\n            ))\n\n        # Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize session manager\n    session_manager = SessionManager()\n    sessions = []\n    session_threads = []\n    session_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    # Start Appium sessions for each device (up to max_concurrent_sessions)\n    max_sessions = min(config.max_concurrent_sessions, len(available_devices))\n    logger.log(f"Starting up to {max_sessions} Appium sessions.", "INFO")\n\n    def start_session_thread(device):\n        try:\n            session = session_manager.start_session(device)\n            with session_lock:\n                sessions.append(session)\n            logger.log(f"Started session {session.session_id} for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n            session_manager.handle_failure(device.device_id, e)\n\n    threads = []\n    for device in available_devices[:max_sessions]:\n        t = threading.Thread(target=start_session_thread, args=(device,))\n        t.start()\n        threads.append(t)\n\n    for t in threads:\n        t.join()\n\n    if not sessions:\n        logger.log("No sessions started successfully. Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize test runner\n    test_runner = TestRunner()\n    test_scripts = config.test_scripts\n\n    # Run tests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor\n    logger.log("Running tests on all sessions.", "INFO")\n    test_results = {}\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_sessions) as executor:\n        future_to_session = {\n            executor.submit(test_runner.run_tests, [session], test_scripts): session.session_id\n            for session in sessions\n        }\n        for future in as_completed(future_to_session):\n            session_id = future_to_session[future]\n            try:\n                result_dict = future.result()\n                test_results.update(result_dict)\n                logger.log(f"Test completed for session {session_id}.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.log_error(e)\n                session_manager.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n\n    # Monitor system metrics and report status\n    monitor = Monitor(sessions)\n    try:\n        metrics = monitor.collect_metrics()\n        monitor.report_status()\n        logger.log(f"System metrics: {metrics}", "INFO")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # from typing import List, Dict
from config import DeviceConfig

class DeviceManager:
    """
    Manages device discovery and availability for multiple Android devices/emulators.
    """

    def __init__(self, devices: List[DeviceConfig]):
        """
        Initialize DeviceManager with a list of DeviceConfig objects.
        """
        self.devices: Dict[str, DeviceConfig] = {device.device_id: device for device in devices}
        self._available_devices: Dict[str, DeviceConfig] = {}

    def discover_devices(self) -> None:
        """
        Discover available devices from the configured device pool.
        For real device discovery, this could be extended to use adb or other mechanisms.
        Here, we assume all configured devices are available.
        """
        # In a real implementation, you might check device connectivity via adb or Appium.
        # For now, we assume all devices in self.devices are available.
        self._available_devices = self.devices.copy()

    def get_available_devices(self) -> List[DeviceConfig]:
        """
        Return a list of available DeviceConfig objects.
        """
        return list(self._available_devices.values())

Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize test runner\n    test_runner = TestRunner()\n    test_scripts = config.test_scripts\n\n    # Run tests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor\n    logger.log("Running tests on all sessions.", "INFO")\n    test_results = {}\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_sessions) as executor:\n        future_to_session = {\n            executor.submit(test_runner.run_tests, [session], test_scripts): session.session_id\n            for session in sessions\n        }\n        for future in as_completed(future_to_session):\n            session_id = future_to_session[future]\n            try:\n                result_dict = future.result()\n                test_results.update(result_dict)\n                logger.log(f"Test completed for session {session_id}.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.log_error(e)\n                session_manager.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n\n    # Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize session manager\n    session_manager = SessionManager()\n    sessions = []\n    session_threads = []\n    session_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    # Start Appium sessions for each device (up to max_concurrent_sessions)\n    max_sessions = min(config.max_concurrent_sessions, len(available_devices))\n    logger.log(f"Starting up to {max_sessions} Appium sessions.", "INFO")\n\n    def start_session_thread(device):\n        try:\n            session = session_manager.start_session(device)\n            with session_lock:\n                sessions.append(session)\n            logger.log(f"Started session {session.session_id} for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n            session_manager.handle_failure(device.device_id, e)\n\n    threads = []\n    for device in available_devices[:max_sessions]:\n        t = threading.Thread(target=start_session_thread, args=(device,))\n        t.start()\n        threads.append(t)\n\n    for t in threads:\n        t.join()\n\n    if not sessions:\n        logger.log("No sessions started successfully. Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize test runner\n    test_runner = TestRunner()\n    test_scripts = config.test_scripts\n\n    # Run tests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor\n    logger.log("Running tests on all sessions.", "INFO")\n    test_results = {}\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_sessions) as executor:\n        future_to_session = {\n            executor.submit(test_runner.run_tests, [session], test_scripts): session.session_id\n            for session in sessions\n        }\n        for future in as_completed(future_to_session):\n            session_id = future_to_session[future]\n            try:\n                result_dict = future.result()\n                test_results.update(result_dict)\n                logger.log(f"Test completed for session {session_id}.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.log_error(e)\n                session_manager.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n\n    # Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/multithreaded_appium_android/session_manager.py has been successfully created, with content:
import threading
import time
import traceback
from typing import Dict, Optional
from appium import webdriver

from config import DeviceConfig
from logger import Logger

class AppiumSession:
    """
    Represents an Appium session for a specific Android device.
    """
    def __init__(self, session_id: str, device: DeviceConfig):
        self.session_id = session_id
        self.device = device
        self.driver: Optional[webdriver.Remote] = None
        self.status = "INITIALIZED"
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def start(self):
        """
        Start the Appium session by initializing the Appium driver.
        """
        desired_caps = {
            "platformName": "Android",
            "platformVersion": self.device.platform_version,
            "deviceName": self.device.model,
            "udid": self.device.device_id,
            "automationName": "UiAutomator2",
            # Add more capabilities as needed
        }
        try:
            self.driver = webdriver.Remote(self.device.appium_url, desired_caps)
            self.status = "RUNNING"
        except Exception as e:
            self.status = "FAILED"
            raise e

    def stop(self):
        """
        Stop the Appium session and quit the driver.
        """
        with self._lock:
            if self.driver:
                try:
                    self.driver.quit()
                except Exception:
                    pass
            self.status = "STOPPED"

    def restart(self):
        """
        Restart the Appium session.
        """
        self.stop()
        time.sleep(2)  # Small delay before restart
        self.start()

class SessionManager:
    """
    Manages Appium sessions for multiple Android devices.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self.sessions: Dict[str, AppiumSession] = {}
        self._lock = threading.Lock()
        self.logger = Logger()

    def start_session(self, device: DeviceConfig) -> AppiumSession:
        """
        Start a new Appium session for the given device.
        """
        session_id = f"{device.device_id}-{int(time.time()*1000)}"
        session = AppiumSession(session_id, device)
        try:
            session.start()
            with self._lock:
                self.sessions[session_id] = session
            self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} started for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")
            return session
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.log_error(e)
            self.handle_failure(session_id, e)
            raise e

    def stop_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:
        """
        Stop the Appium session with the given session_id.
        """
        with self._lock:
            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)
        if session:
            try:
                session.stop()
                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} stopped.", "INFO")
            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.log_error(e)
            with self._lock:
                del self.sessions[session_id]

    def restart_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[AppiumSession]:
        """
        Restart the Appium session with the given session_id.
        """
        with self._lock:
            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)
        if session:
            try:
                session.restart()
                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} restarted.", "INFO")
                return session
            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.log_error(e)
                self.handle_failure(session_id, e)
        return None

    def handle_failure(self, session_id: str, error: Exception) -> None:
        """
        Handle session failure, log error, and attempt recovery if possible.
        """
        self.logger.log(f"Handling failure for session {session_id}: {str(error)}", "ERROR")
        self.logger.log_error(error)
        # Attempt to restart session if it exists
        with self._lock:
            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)
        if session and session.status != "STOPPED":
            try:
                session.restart()
                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} recovered after failure.", "INFO")
            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} could not be recovered: {str(e)}", "ERROR")
                session.stop()
                with self._lock:
                    del self.sessions[session_id]

Appium driver.\n        """\n        desired_caps = {\n            "platformName": "Android",\n            "platformVersion": self.device.platform_version,\n            "deviceName": self.driver.quit()\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n            self.status = "STOPPED"\n\n    def restart(self):\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session.\n        """\n        self.stop()\n        time.sleep(2)  # Small delay before restart\n        self.start()\n\nclass SessionManager:\n    """\n    Manages Appium sessions for multiple Android devices.\n self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n            raise e\n\n    def stop_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n {session_id} stopped.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n            with self._lock:\n                del self.sessions[session_id]\n\n    def restart_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[AppiumSession]:\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} restarted.", "INFO")\n handle_failure(self, session_id: str, error: Exception) -> None:\n        """\n        Handle session failure, log error, and attempt recovery if possible.\n        """\n        self.logger.log(f"Handling failure for session {session_id}: {str(error)}", "ERROR")\n        self.logger.log_error(error)\n        # Attempt to restart session if it exists\n        with self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} recovered after failure.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} could not be recovered: {str(e)}", Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n            raise e\n\n    def stop_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} restarted.", "INFO")\n Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize session manager\n    session_manager = SessionManager()\n    sessions = []\n    session_threads = []\n    session_lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    # Start Appium sessions for each device (up to max_concurrent_sessions)\n    max_sessions = min(config.max_concurrent_sessions, len(available_devices))\n    logger.log(f"Starting up to {max_sessions} Appium sessions.", "INFO")\n\n    def start_session_thread(device):\n        try:\n            session = session_manager.start_session(device)\n            with session_lock:\n                sessions.append(session)\n            logger.log(f"Started session {session.session_id} for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n            session_manager.handle_failure(device.device_id, e)\n\n    threads = []\n    for device in available_devices[:max_sessions]:\n        t = threading.Thread(target=start_session_thread, args=(device,))\n        t.start()\n        threads.append(t)\n\n    for t in threads:\n        t.join()\n\n    if not sessions:\n        logger.log("No sessions started successfully. Exiting.", "ERROR")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    # Initialize test runner\n    test_runner = TestRunner()\n    test_scripts = config.test_scripts\n\n    # Run tests in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor\n    logger.log("Running tests on all sessions.", "INFO")\n    test_results = {}\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_sessions) as executor:\n        future_to_session = {\n            executor.submit(test_runner.run_tests, [session], test_scripts): session.session_id\n            for session in sessions\n        }\n        for future in as_completed(future_to_session):\n            session_id = future_to_session[future]\n            try:\n                result_dict = future.result()\n                test_results.update(result_dict)\n                logger.log(f"Test completed for session {session_id}.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.log_error(e)\n                session_manager.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n\n    # Monitor system metrics and report status\n    monitor = Monitor(sessions)\n    try:\n        metrics = monitor.collect_metrics()\n        monitor.report_status()\n        logger.log(f"System metrics: {metrics}", "INFO")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # Print summary of test results\n    logger.log("Test Results Summary:", "INFO")\n    for session_id, result in test_results.items():\n        logger.log(\n            f"Session {session_id}: Success={result.success}, Details={result.details}, Timestamp={result.timestamp}",\n            "INFO"\n        )\n\n    logger.log("Multithreaded Appium automation system finished.", "INFO")\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    main()'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of main.py is complete and matches the requirements outlined in the current task. self.driver = webdriver.Remote(self.device.appium_url, desired_caps)\n            self.status = "RUNNING"\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.status = "FAILED"\n            raise e\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session and quit the driver.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            if self.driver:\n                try:\n                    self.driver.quit()\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n            self.status = "STOPPED"\n\n    def restart(self):\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session.\n        """\n        self.stop()\n        time.sleep(2)  # Small delay before restart\n        self.start()\n\nclass SessionManager:\n    """\n    Manages Appium sessions for multiple Android devices.\n    """\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.sessions: Dict[str, AppiumSession] = {}\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n        self.logger = Logger()\n\n    def start_session(self, device: DeviceConfig) -> AppiumSession:\n        """\n        Start a new Appium session for the given device.\n        """\n        session_id = f"{device.device_id}-{int(time.time()*1000)}"\n        session = AppiumSession(session_id, device)\n        try:\n            session.start()\n            with self._lock:\n                self.sessions[session_id] = session\n            self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} started for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n            return session\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.log_error(e)\n            self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n            raise e\n\n    def stop_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.stop()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} stopped.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n            with self._lock:\n                del self.sessions[session_id]\n\n    def restart_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[AppiumSession]:\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} restarted.", "INFO")\n                return session\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n                self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n        return None\n\n    def handle_failure(self, session_id: str, error: Exception) -> None:\n        """\n        Handle session failure, log error, and attempt recovery if possible.\n        """\n        self.logger.log(f"Handling failure for session {session_id}: {str(error)}", "ERROR")\n        self.logger.log_error(error)\n        # Attempt to restart session if it exists\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session and session.status != "STOPPED":\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} recovered after failure.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} could not be recovered: {str(e)}", "ERROR")\n                session.stop()\n                with self._lock:\n                    del self.sessions[session_id]'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/multithreaded_appium_android/test_runner.py has been successfully created, with content:
import importlib.util
import os
import traceback
from typing import List, Dict

from session_manager import AppiumSession
from logger import Logger
from datetime import datetime

class TestResult:
    def __init__(self, session_id: str, success: bool, details: str, timestamp: datetime):
        self.session_id = session_id
        self.success = success
        self.details = details
        self.timestamp = timestamp

    def __repr__(self):
        return (f"TestResult(session_id={self.session_id}, success={self.success}, "
                f"details={self.details}, timestamp={self.timestamp})")

class TestRunner:
    """
    Executes UI interaction scripts on each AppiumSession and collects results.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self.logger = Logger()

    def run_tests(self, sessions: List[AppiumSession], test_scripts: List[str]) -> Dict[str, TestResult]:
        """
        Run the provided test scripts on each AppiumSession.
        Returns a dictionary mapping session_id to TestResult.
        """
        results = {}
        for session in sessions:
            session_result = self._run_session_tests(session, test_scripts)
            results[session.session_id] = session_result
        return results

    def _run_session_tests(self, session: AppiumSession, test_scripts: List[str]) -> TestResult:
        """
        Run all test scripts on a single AppiumSession.
        Each test script should be a Python file with a 'run_test(driver)' function.
        """
        success = True
        details = ""
        for script_path in test_scripts:
            try:
                if not os.path.isfile(script_path):
                    raise FileNotFoundError(f"Test script not found: {script_path}")

                # Dynamically import the test script as a module
                module_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(script_path))[0]
                spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, script_path)
                test_module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
                spec.loader.exec_module(test_module)

                # Run the test function
                if not hasattr(test_module, "run_test"):
                    raise AttributeError(f"Test script {script_path} does not have a 'run_test(driver)' function.")

                self.logger.log(f"Running test '{script_path}' on session {session.session_id}", "INFO")
                test_module.run_test(session.driver)
                details += f"Test '{script_path}': PASSED\n"
            except Exception as e:
                success = False
                error_msg = f"Test '{script_path}': FAILED - {str(e)}\n{traceback.format_exc()}\n"
                details += error_msg
                self.logger.log_error(e)
        timestamp = datetime.now()
        return TestResult(session.session_id, success, details, timestamp)

Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/multithreaded_appium_android/session_manager.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport threading\nimport time\nimport traceback\nfrom typing import Dict, Optional\nfrom appium import webdriver\n\nfrom config import DeviceConfig\nfrom logger import Logger\n\nclass AppiumSession:\n    """\n    Represents an Appium session for a specific Android device.\n    """\n    def __init__(self, session_id: str, device: DeviceConfig):\n        self.session_id = session_id\n        self.device = device\n        self.driver: Optional[webdriver.Remote] = None\n        self.status = "INITIALIZED"\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    def start(self):\n        """\n        Start the Appium session by initializing the Appium driver.\n        """\n        desired_caps = {\n            "platformName": "Android",\n            "platformVersion": self.device.platform_version,\n            "deviceName": self.device.model,\n            "udid": self.device.device_id,\n            "automationName": "UiAutomator2",\n            # Add more capabilities as needed\n        }\n        try:\n            self.driver = webdriver.Remote(self.device.appium_url, desired_caps)\n            self.status = "RUNNING"\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.status = "FAILED"\n            raise e\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session and quit the driver.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            if self.driver:\n                try:\n                    self.driver.quit()\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n            self.status = "STOPPED"\n\n    def restart(self):\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session.\n        """\n        self.stop()\n        time.sleep(2)  # Small delay before restart\n        self.start()\n\nclass SessionManager:\n    """\n    Manages Appium sessions for multiple Android devices.\n    """\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.sessions: Dict[str, AppiumSession] = {}\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n        self.logger = Logger()\n\n    def start_session(self, device: DeviceConfig) -> AppiumSession:\n        """\n        Start a new Appium session for the given device.\n        """\n        session_id = f"{device.device_id}-{int(time.time()*1000)}"\n        session = AppiumSession(session_id, device)\n        try:\n            session.start()\n            with self._lock:\n                self.sessions[session_id] = session\n            self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} started for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n            return session\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.log_error(e)\n            self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n            raise e\n\n    def stop_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.stop()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} stopped.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n            with self._lock:\n                del self.sessions[session_id]\n\n    def restart_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[AppiumSession]:\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} restarted.", "INFO")\n                return session\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n                self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n        return None\n\n    def Stop all sessions\n    logger.log("Stopping all sessions.", "INFO")\n    for session in sessions:\n        try:\n            session_manager.stop_session(session.session_id)\n            logger.log(f"Stopped session {session.session_id}.", "INFO")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.log_error(e)\n\n    # /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/multithreaded_appium_android/session_manager.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport threading\nimport time\nimport traceback\nfrom typing import Dict, Optional\nfrom appium import webdriver\n\nfrom config import DeviceConfig\nfrom logger import Logger\n\nclass AppiumSession:\n    """\n    Represents an Appium session for a specific Android device.\n    """\n    def __init__(self, session_id: str, device: DeviceConfig):\n        self.session_id = session_id\n        self.device = device\n        self.driver: Optional[webdriver.Remote] = None\n        self.status = "INITIALIZED"\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    def start(self):\n        """\n        Start the Appium session by initializing the Appium driver.\n        """\n        desired_caps = {\n            "platformName": "Android",\n            "platformVersion": self.device.platform_version,\n            "deviceName": self.device.model,\n            "udid": self.device.device_id,\n            "automationName": "UiAutomator2",\n            # Add more capabilities as needed\n        }\n        try:\n            self.driver = webdriver.Remote(self.device.appium_url, desired_caps)\n            self.status = "RUNNING"\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.status = "FAILED"\n            raise e\n\n    def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session and quit the driver.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            if self.driver:\n                try:\n                    self.driver.quit()\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n            self.status = "STOPPED"\n\n    def restart(self):\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session.\n        """\n        self.stop()\n        time.sleep(2)  # Small delay before restart\n        self.start()\n\nclass SessionManager:\n    """\n    Manages Appium sessions for multiple Android devices.\n    """\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.sessions: Dict[str, AppiumSession] = {}\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n        self.logger = Logger()\n\n    def start_session(self, device: DeviceConfig) -> AppiumSession:\n        """\n        Start a new Appium session for the given device.\n        """\n        session_id = f"{device.device_id}-{int(time.time()*1000)}"\n        session = AppiumSession(session_id, device)\n        try:\n            session.start()\n            with self._lock:\n                self.sessions[session_id] = session\n            self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} started for device {device.device_id}", "INFO")\n            return session\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.log_error(e)\n            self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n            raise e\n\n    def stop_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.stop()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} stopped.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n            with self._lock:\n                del self.sessions[session_id]\n\n    def restart_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[AppiumSession]:\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} restarted.", "INFO")\n                return session\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n                self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n        return None\n\n    def handle_failure(self, session_id: str, error: Exception) -> None:\n        """\n        Handle session failure, log error, and attempt recovery if possible.\n        """\n        self.logger.log(f"Handling failure for session {session_id}: {str(error)}", "ERROR")\n        self.logger.log_error(error)\n        # Attempt to restart session if it exists\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session and session.status != "STOPPED":\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} recovered after failure.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} could not be recovered: {str(e)}", "ERROR")\n                session.stop()\n                with self._lock:\n                    del self.sessions[session_id]'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of """\n    Executes UI interaction scripts on each AppiumSession and collects results.\n    """\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = Logger()\n\n    def run_tests(self, sessions: TestResult.\n        """\n        results = {}\n        for session in sessions:\n            session_result = self._run_session_tests(session, test_scripts)\n            results[session.session_id] = AppiumSession.\n        Each test script should be a Python file with a \'run_test(driver)\' function.\n        """\n        success = True\n        details = ""\n        for script_path in test_scripts:\n            try:\n                if not os.path.isfile(script_path):\n                    raise FileNotFoundError(f"Test script not found: {script_path}")\n\n                # Dynamically import the test script as a module\n                module_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(script_path))[0]\n                spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, script_path)\n                test_module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)\n                spec.loader.exec_module(test_module)\n\n                # Run the test function\n                if not hasattr(test_module, "run_test"):\n                    raise AttributeError(f"Test script {script_path} does not have a \'run_test(driver)\' function.")\n\n                self.logger.log(f"Running test \'{script_path}\' on session {session.session_id}", "INFO")\n                test_module.run_test(session.driver)\n                details import psutil
import threading
import time
from typing import List, Dict, Any

from session_manager import AppiumSession
from logger import Logger

class Monitor:
    """
    Collects resource metrics, reports status of Appium sessions, and logs events in real time.
    """

    def __init__(self, sessions: List[AppiumSession], interval: float = 5.0):
        """
        Initialize Monitor with a list of AppiumSession objects and monitoring interval (seconds).
        """
        self.sessions = sessions
        self.interval = interval
        self.logger = Logger()
        self._stop_event = threading.Event()
        self._monitor_thread = None
        self._metrics: Dict[str, Any] = {}

    def collect_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Collect system resource metrics (CPU, memory, etc.).
        Returns a dictionary of metrics.
        """
        metrics = {
            "cpu_percent": psutil.cpu_percent(interval=1),
            "memory_percent": psutil.virtual_memory().percent,
            "disk_percent": psutil.disk_usage('/').percent,
            "net_io": psutil.net_io_counters()._asdict(),
            "active_sessions": len(self.sessions),
            "session_statuses": {s.session_id: s.status for s in self.sessions}
        }
        self._metrics = metrics
        self.logger.log(f"Collected system metrics: {metrics}", "DEBUG")
        return metrics

    def report_status(self) -> None:
        """
        Report the status of all Appium sessions and system metrics.
        """
        self.logger.log("Reporting Appium session statuses:", "INFO")
        for session in self.sessions:
            self.logger.log(
                f"Session {session.session_id}: Device={session.device.device_id}, Status={session.status}",
                "INFO"
            )
        self.logger.log(f"System Metrics: {self._metrics}", "INFO")

    def log_event(self, event: str) -> None:
        """
        Log a custom event.
        """
        self.logger.log(f"Monitor Event: {event}", "INFO")

    def start_monitoring(self) -> None:
        """
        Start real-time monitoring in a background thread.
        """
        if self._monitor_thread and self._monitor_thread.is_alive():
            self.logger.log("Monitor is already running.", "WARNING")
            return

        self._stop_event.clear()
        self._monitor_thread = threading.Thread(target=self._monitor_loop, daemon=True)
        self._monitor_thread.start()
        self.logger.log("Started real-time monitoring thread.", "INFO")

    def stop_monitoring(self) -> None:
        """
        Stop the real-time monitoring thread.
        """
        self._stop_event.set()
        if self._monitor_thread:
            self._monitor_thread.join()
            self.logger.log("Stopped real-time monitoring thread.", "INFO")

    def _monitor_loop(self) -> None:
        """
        Internal loop for periodic monitoring.
        """
        while not self._stop_event.is_set():
            try:
                self.collect_metrics()
                self.report_status()
            except Exception as e:
                self.logger.log_error(e)
            time.sleep(self.interval)

Optional[webdriver.Remote] = None\n        self.status = "INITIALIZED"\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    def start(self):\n        """\n        Start the Appium session by initializing the Appium driver.\n        """\n        desired_caps = {\n            "platformName": "Android",\n            "platformVersion": self.device.platform_version,\n            "deviceName": self.device.model,\n            "udid": self.device.device_id,\n            "automationName": "UiAutomator2",\n            # Add more capabilities as needed\n        }\n        try:\n  def stop(self):\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session and quit the driver.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            if self.driver:\n                try:\n                    self.driver.quit()\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n            self.status = "STOPPED"\n\n    def restart(self):\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session.\n        """\n        self.stop()\n        time.sleep(2)  # Small delay before restart\n        self.start()\n\nclass SessionManager:\n    """\n    Manages Appium sessions for multiple Android devices.\n    """\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.sessions: Dict[str, AppiumSession] = {}\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n        self.logger = Logger()\n\n    def start_session(self, device: DeviceConfig) -> AppiumSession:\n        """\n        Start a new Appium session for the given device.\n        """\n        session_id = f"{device.device_id}-{int(time.time()*1000)}"\n        session = AppiumSession(session_id, device)\n        try:\n            session.start()\n            with self._lock:\n                self.sessions[session_id] = session\n            self.logger.log(f"Session self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n            raise e\n\n    def stop_session(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        """\n        Stop the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.stop()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} stopped.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n            with self._lock:\n                del self.sessions[session_id]\n\n    def restart_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[AppiumSession]:\n        """\n        Restart the Appium session with the given session_id.\n        """\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session:\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} restarted.", "INFO")\n                return session\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n                self.handle_failure(session_id, e)\n        return None\n\n    def handle_failure(self, session_id: str, error: Exception) -> None:\n        """\n        Handle session failure, log error, and attempt recovery if possible.\n        """\n        self.logger.log(f"Handling failure for session {session_id}: {str(error)}", "ERROR")\n        self.logger.log_error(error)\n        # Attempt to restart session if it exists\n        with self._lock:\n            session = self.sessions.get(session_id)\n        if session and session.status != "STOPPED":\n            try:\n                session.restart()\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} recovered after failure.", "INFO")\n            except Exception as e:\n                self.logger.log(f"Session {session_id} could not be recovered: {str(e)}", bool, details: str, timestamp: datetime):\n        self.session_id = session_id\n        self.success = success\n        self.details = details\n        self.timestamp = timestamp\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return (f"TestResult(session_id={self.session_id}, success={self.success}, "\n                f"details={self.details}, timestamp={self.timestamp})")\n\nclass TestRunner:\n    """\n    Executes UI interaction scripts on each AppiumSession and collects results.\n    """\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = Logger()\n\n    def run_tests(self, sessions: List[AppiumSession], test_scripts: List[str]) -> Dict[str, TestResult]:\n        """\n        Run the provided test scripts on each AppiumSession.\n        Returns a dictionary mapping session_id to TestResult.\n        """\n        results = {}\n        for session in sessions:\n            session_result = self._run_session_tests(session, test_scripts)\n            results[session.session_id] = session_result\n        return results\n\n    def _run_session_tests(self, session: AppiumSession, test_scripts: List[str]) -> TestResult:\n        """\n        Run all test scripts on a single AppiumSession.\n        Each test script should be a Python file with a \'run_test(driver)\' function.\n        """\n        success = True\n        details = ""\n        for script_path in test_scripts:\n            try:\n                if not os.path.isfile(script_path):\n                    raise FileNotFoundError(f"Test script not found: {script_path}")\n\n                # Dynamically import the test script as a module\n                module_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(script_path))[0]\n                spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, script_path)\n                test_module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)\n                spec.loader.exec_module(test_module)\n\n                # Run the test function\n                if not hasattr(test_module, "run_test"):\n                    raise AttributeError(f"Test script {script_path} does not have a \'run_test(driver)\' function.")\n\n                self.logger.log(f"Running test \'{script_path}\' on session {session.session_id}", "INFO")\n                test_module.run_test(session.driver)\n                details += f"Test \'{script_path}\': PASSED\\n"\n            except Exception as e:\n                success = False\n                error_msg = f"Test \'{script_path}\': FAILED - {str(e)}\\n{traceback.format_exc()}\\n"\n                details += error_msg\n                self.logger.log_error(e)\n        timestamp = datetime.now()\n        return
